Package io.github.senthilganeshs.fj.ds

Class Either.Left<A,B>

java.lang.Object
io.github.senthilganeshs.fj.ds.Either.Left<A,B>

    • empty

      public <R> Collection<R> empty()
      Description copied from interface: Collection
      Returns an empty instance of the data structure.
      Specified by:
      empty in interface Collection<A>
      Type Parameters:
      R - The type of elements for the empty collection.
      Returns:
      An empty Collection implementation.

    • foldl

      public <R> R foldl(R seed, BiFunction<R,B,R> fn)
      Description copied from interface: Collection
      Performs a left-associative reduction (fold) over the elements of this collection. This is the primary traversal mechanism for all derived operations.
      Specified by:
      foldl in interface Collection<A>
      Type Parameters:
      R - The type of the accumulated result.
      Parameters:
      seed - The initial value for the accumulation.
      fn - The combining function (accumulator, element) -> result.
      Returns:
      The final accumulated value.

    • build

      public Either<A,B> build(B value)
      Description copied from interface: Collection
      Creates a new instance of the data structure with the provided element added. This operation is persistent and does not modify the original instance.
      Specified by:
      build in interface Collection<A>
      Specified by:
      build in interface Either<A,B>
      Parameters:
      value - The element to add.
      Returns:
      A new Collection containing the added element.

    • either

      public <R> R either(Function<A,R> fa, Function<B,R> fb)
      Description copied from interface: Either
      Applies either the left function or the right function to the contained value.
      Specified by:
      either in interface Either<A,B>
      Type Parameters:
      R - The resulting type.
      Parameters:
      fa - Function to apply if this is Left.
      fb - Function to apply if this is Right.
      Returns:
      The result of applying the appropriate function.
